more.title: Duo up for killing bar patron, injuring two others
more.description: POLICE in Ruwa situated on the outskirts of Harare have arrested two men for allegedly fatally assaulting an imbiber at a local bar and injuring two others following a dispute over an undisclosed matter. more.description: THE Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has hailed the general public for supporting law enforcement agencies in tackling the menace of drugs and substance misuse. The plaudits come in the wake of tips from members of the public that led to the arrest of two drug peddlers and their subsequent court appearance, conviction and sentencing. more.title: ILO commends Zimbabwean workers for resilience in wake of hardships
more.description: THE International Labour Organisation (ILO) has commended Zimbabwean workers for demonstrating resilience in the face of obtaining economic hardships. The remarks were made during the Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) May 1 commemorations at Dzivarasekwa Stadium in Harare this Friday. Addressing workers, the ILO country director for Zimbabwe, Philile Masuku, said the country’s workers [...] The post ILO commends Zimbabwean workers for resilience in wake of hardships appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: NSSA processed 12,400 compensation claims from workplace injuries last year: Government
more.description: THE National Social Security Authority (NSSA) processed compensation claims from a total of 12,400 workplace injuries last year alone, a development which reflects the country’s occupational and safety gaps. In 2022, Zimbabwe recorded 4,912 workplace injuries and 70 fatalities. While injuries dropped to 4,334 in 2023 with 60 deaths, the trend failed to sustain. In [...] The post NSSA processed 12,400 compensation claims from workplace injuries last year: Government appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Corrupt cartels blamed for worsening plight of Zimbabwe’s working class
more.description: THE Zimbabwe Congress of Trade Unions (ZCTU) has slammed corrupt cartels for fuelling workers’ hardships amid calls for the enactment of pro-poor policies.
